subject: Geography
title: Folded Mountains
description: A quick win: understand Folded Mountains.

hook.text: Push a tablecloth from both sides and it wrinkles. That's how folded mountains form.

buildup.text: Layers of sedimentary rock, compressed over millions of years, buckle into ridges.

discovery.text: The Appalachians were once taller than the Himalayas, formed 480 million years ago.

twist.text: Time wore them down. The Appalachians are now gentle hills compared to their former glory.

climax.text: Fossils of sea creatures sit atop the Himalayas—proof those rocks were once ocean floor.

punchline.text: Seashells on mountaintops tell the whole story.

quiz.question: What evidence proves Himalayan rocks were once ocean floor?
options: Marine fossils found at the summit
